source.book: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析7》

segments.text: For my presentation today, I will focus on earth-sheltered housing or underground housing as it's more commonly known.
segments.translation: 今天的演讲中，我将重点介绍覆土住宅，也就是更常说的地下住宅。

segments.text: I'll illustrate how it can meet the demands of ecological environmental planning regarding building and energy resources.
segments.translation: 我将说明它如何在建筑和能源资源方面满足生态环境规划的要求。

segments.text: Let's first look at the advantages of earth-sheltered housing.
segments.translation: 我们先来看看覆土住宅的优点。

segments.text: Perhaps the most significant merit of earth-sheltered housing is the low impact on the environment since houses built underground can hardly be seen from the surface.
segments.translation: 覆土住宅最显著的优点或许是对环境影响很小，因为从地面上几乎看不到建在地下的房屋。

segments.text: Then, earth-sheltered houses are not only environmentally friendly but also surprisingly efficient regarding energy.
segments.translation: 此外，覆土住宅不仅环保，而且在能源利用方面效率高得惊人。

segments.text: It is because underground housing can make great use of the alternative technologies to improve energy efficiency.
segments.translation: 这是因为地下住宅可以充分利用替代技术来提高能源效率。

segments.text: Moreover, many earth-sheltered housing projects use natural, non-toxic building materials.
segments.translation: 此外，许多覆土住宅项目使用天然、无毒的建筑材料。

segments.text: Some are even more durable than standard building materials.
segments.translation: 有些材料甚至比标准建筑材料更加耐用。

segments.text: As a result, earth-sheltered houses have a lifespan of 500 years or more.
segments.translation: 因此，覆土住宅的使用寿命可达500年或更久。

segments.text: This type of building technique means less exposure to the outside elements like sunshine, storm, snow etc., so it can give the houses actual protection and keep the maintenance of the houses to the minimum.
segments.translation: 这种建筑技术意味着房屋较少暴露在阳光、暴风雨、降雪等外界自然因素中，因此能为房屋提供切实的保护，并将房屋维护需求降到最低。

segments.text: Earth-sheltered houses also give people a quiet environment.
segments.translation: 覆土住宅还能为人们提供安静的环境。

segments.text: As we know, noise is a severe problem in modern society, and many people consider moving home because of this.
segments.translation: 众所周知，噪声是现代社会的一个严重问题，许多人因此考虑搬家。

segments.text: In that case, earth-sheltered houses might be a good choice because living underground means those people will no longer suffer from the disturbances caused by the traffic on the road.
segments.translation: 在这种情况下，覆土住宅或许是不错的选择，因为住在地下意味着人们不再受到道路交通的干扰。

segments.text: And lastly, earth-sheltered houses also bring significant health advantages to people.
segments.translation: 最后，覆土住宅还会给人们的健康带来显著益处。

segments.text: Because of the sophistication of the ventilation systems installed in earth-sheltered houses, it provides a comfortable living environment for people who have asthma and other chest diseases.
segments.translation: 由于覆土住宅安装了先进的通风系统，它能为哮喘和其他胸部疾病患者提供舒适的居住环境。

segments.text: Then let's move on to earth-sheltered housing in the UK.
segments.translation: 接下来，我们来看看英国的覆土住宅。

segments.text: Despite all the apparent advantages we mentioned above, earth-sheltered housing still isn't a popular choice for most British families, as the statistics show.
segments.translation: 尽管它具有我们上面提到的种种明显优势，但统计数据显示，对大多数英国家庭而言，覆土住宅仍不是热门选择。

segments.text: The British Earth-sheltering Association tries to persuade people to try this type of accommodation, saying that it's easier to get planning permission for an earth-sheltered house.
segments.translation: 英国覆土住宅协会努力说服人们尝试这种住宅，并表示覆土住宅更容易获得规划许可。

segments.text: In contrast, for a conventional home, there is always too much red tape.
segments.translation: 相比之下，建造传统住宅总是有太多繁文缛节。

segments.text: A lot of evidence shows that the building development offices are more sympathetic to earth-sheltered projects, especially in the conservation areas.
segments.translation: 大量证据表明，建筑开发部门对覆土住宅项目更为支持，尤其是在保护区内。

segments.text: On the sites where conventional houses aren't usually allowed to build, earth-sheltered housing might be a feasible solution.
segments.translation: 在通常不允许建造传统住宅的地块上，覆土住宅可能是一种可行的解决方案。

segments.text: And there is even a possibility that one day, earth-sheltered houses can take the place of mobile homes along the coastlines in the UK.
segments.translation: 甚至有可能在未来某一天，覆土住宅会取代英国海岸线一带的活动房屋。

segments.text: In the last part, I'd like to talk about two different projects in the UK: Mole Manor and Hockerton.
segments.translation: 最后一部分，我想介绍英国的两个不同项目：Mole Manor和Hockerton。

segments.text: When it was first built, Mole Manor attracted wide publicity around the world.
segments.translation: Mole Manor刚建成时，便在世界各地引起了媒体的广泛关注。

segments.text: A newspaper poll conducted by the Earth-sheltering Association showed that Mole Manor was voted as one of the twelve most desirable places to live in the world.
segments.translation: 覆土住宅协会开展的一项报纸民意调查显示，Mole Manor被评为全球最理想的十二个居住地之一。

segments.text: Similarly, Hockerton aroused considerable interest from the international media as well, winning several awards for its innovative environmental design.
segments.translation: 同样，Hockerton也引起了国际媒体的广泛关注，并凭借创新的环保设计赢得了多个奖项。

segments.text: The running cost for Mole Manor is much lower than that of conventional houses.
segments.translation: Mole Manor的日常运营成本远低于传统住宅。

segments.text: It is because Mole Manor is installed with an effective insulation system, significantly reducing its heating bill.
segments.translation: 这是因为Mole Manor安装了高效的隔热系统，大幅降低了取暖费用。

segments.text: While at Hockerton, all the basic needs of families living there are satisfied by energies in this area.
segments.translation: 而在Hockerton，住户家庭的所有基本需求都由当地的能源来满足。

segments.text: For example, wind power, solar energy and rain are used to generate enough warmth, water for washing and drinking, and electricity for lighting and cooking.
segments.translation: 例如，利用风能、太阳能和雨水来提供充足的热量、洗涤和饮用水，以及照明和烹饪所需的电力。

segments.text: As a result, people there can live a lifestyle in harmony with the environment.
segments.translation: 因此，那里的人们可以过上与环境和谐共处的生活。

segments.text: It is also proved that at Hockerton, earth-sheltered houses can be built on a modest budget because it gives priority to cost when designing the houses.
segments.translation: 事实还证明，Hockerton的覆土住宅可以用适中的预算建成，因为房屋设计时会优先考虑成本。

segments.text: But it is not the case with Mole Manor, which pays more attention to style than cost.
segments.translation: 但Mole Manor并非如此，它比起成本更注重风格。

segments.text: Compared with the homely cottage style at Hockerton, the houses at Mole Manor are expensive, beautiful and comfortable, and it is said that these houses are worth more than 800,000 pounds today.
segments.translation: 与Hockerton朴实温馨的乡间小屋风格相比，Mole Manor的房屋昂贵、美观且舒适，据说如今这些房屋价值超过80万英镑。

segments.text: In conclusion, Mole Manor and Hockerton Projects show that earth-sheltered housing has numerous advantages.
segments.translation: 总而言之，Mole Manor和Hockerton项目表明，覆土住宅具有众多优点。

segments.text: They can not only provide comfortable and stylish houses to people but are also beneficial to the environment.
segments.translation: 它们不仅能为人们提供舒适而时尚的住宅，还有益于环境。

segments.text: And with the help of new energy-saving technology, it can meet all the criteria for ecological and environmental planning.
segments.translation: 借助新的节能技术，覆土住宅可以满足生态与环境规划的所有标准。
